Head north along Fifth Avenue, past the church, and cross at the crosswalk.
Turn left at the intersection, and the museum is around the corner, on your right.
It is across from a small park, next to a bookstore. You cannot miss it.
Turn left at the traffic light, then head north.
Cross at 53rd Street and it is on your right.
It is across from the park, next to a bookstore.
Walk along Fifth for two blocks, past the church.
Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the Museum of Modern Art? I think I am a little lost.
Of course. It is close, about a ten-minute walk. Do you see the traffic light at the end of this block?
Yes, I can see it. Should I cross there?
Exactly. Cross at the crosswalk, then head north on Fifth Avenue and stay on the sidewalk.
Okay, cross at the light, then go straight along Fifth Avenue. How far?
Two blocks. When you reach the intersection with 53rd Street, turn left. The museum is around the corner, on your right.
Is there a landmark I can look for? I do not want to walk past it.
Good question. It is right across from a small park, and there is usually a line of pedestrians on the sidewalk out front.
That is perfect. So, cross at the light, head up Fifth for two blocks, turn left, and it is across from the park.
You have got it. If you get lost, just ask again. New Yorkers are friendlier than people think. Enjoy the museum!
Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the museum?
Is it far, or can I walk there?
Should I cross at the crosswalk?
Is there a landmark I can look for?
So it is around the corner, across from the park. Thank you!

Sure, I can help you get to the Museum of Modern Art. It's not far at all, about a ten-minute walk from your hotel. So, from the front door here, turn right and head north on Fifth Avenue. Keep walking straight, and you'll pass a big church on your right and a bookstore on your left. When you reach the intersection with 53rd Street, you'll see a traffic light and a crosswalk. Cross Fifth Avenue there, and turn left onto 53rd. The museum is about halfway down the block, on your right, across from a small park. You really can't miss it. There's always a line of people on the sidewalk out front. If you get lost, just look for the tall glass tower on the corner. The museum is one block east of there. Oh, and one tip: stay on the sidewalk and watch the crosswalks, because the traffic here moves fast and the drivers don't wait for pedestrians. Have a great visit.

Hi Lucimara, it's Emma. I'm so glad we're having dinner tonight. Let me give you directions to the restaurant, because it's a little hidden and the map can be confusing. Okay, so when you come out of the station at Bedford Avenue, walk toward the water. That's west. Go straight for two blocks along Bedford until you reach a bakery on the corner. The restaurant is right around the corner from the bakery, on a quiet side street. It's next to a flower shop, opposite a small bookstore. If you pass the big painting of a whale on the wall, you've gone one block too far, so just turn around. The whole walk is about five minutes, all on the sidewalk, with no big streets to cross. Text me when you're close and I'll wave from the door. See you at eight.
